methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Field AI, founded by Ali Agha, is a Series A-stage AI & Software company currently valued at $2.0B post-money, having raised $405M to date. The peer universe comprises ten same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies including both private and public comparables. The public universe includes Palantir (PLTR), while notable private peers span Liquid AI, Twelve Labs, OpenAI, and xAI, with valuations ranging from $250M to $300.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $4.0B. Peer-median analysis across the ten-company cohort produced a median valuation of $3.7B, with substantial dispersion ($250M to $300.0B) reflecting the early-stage, high-variance nature of frontier AI. The wide peer range reflects stage and business-model heterogeneity within the AI sector.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $3.1B to $4.6B, with a median of $3.8B. This implies upside to the current $2.0B valuation of approximately 90 percent at the median. Key uncertainties include execution risk inherent to Series A AI businesses, significant valuation dispersion among public and private peers, and sensitivity to stage-progression assumptions in a sector where funding patterns remain volatile.
